title: acilepis thwaitesii
intro: Acilepis thwaitesii, also known as the Wild Dahoon or Wild Jasmine, is a shrub-like plant species native to Sri Lanka. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acilepis thwaitesii are believed to include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity against certain pathogens. Acilepis thwaitesii can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
